questions.text: A subform is contained by:
questions.subject: Computer
questions.explanation: Answer:(A) Parent tableExplanation:A subform is a form that is embedded within another form, called the main form. The subform typically displays data related to the main form's record. For instance, an order form might have a subform that displays all the order details for a specific order.Here's why the other options are incorrect:Resultant table: A resultant table is the output of a join operation between two or more tables in a database. It doesn't contain subforms.Child table: In relational databases, a child table is linked to a parent table through a foreign key relationship. Child tables don't contain subforms.Merge table: A merge table is a temporary table created during the process of merging data from two or more tables. It doesn't contain subforms

questions.text: Which of the following is shortcut key is used to move on next field?
questions.subject: Computer
questions.explanation: Answer: The shortcut key to move to the next field is (A) Tab.Explanation of Wrong Options:(B) Esc: The Esc key is typically used to exit menus or dialog boxes, not to move between fields.(C) F1: The F1 key is generally used to access help menus or documentation within a program.(D) Shift + F2: This key combination may have different functions depending on the specific program you are using. However, it is not commonly used to move between fields

questions.text: Which of the following buttons is used to select one option at a time?
questions.subject: Computer
questions.explanation: Answer: A. Radio buttonExplanation:Radio buttons are used to select one option from a group of mutually exclusive options. This means that only one radio button in a group can be selected at a time. When one radio button is selected, any other radio buttons in the group are automatically deselected.Option buttons is not a standard term for any button used in user interfaces. It's possible that the question intended to say "Radio button".List boxes are used to display a list of options from which users can select one or more options. Unlike radio buttons, list boxes allow users to select multiple options at the same time.Checkboxes are used to select one or more options from a set of options. They are not mutually exclusive, so users can select any combination of checkboxes in a group.In conclusion, radio buttons are the most appropriate choice for selecting only one option at a time

questions.text: Which of the following is not a displayed value while entering?
questions.subject: Computer
questions.explanation: The answer is:(D) Text fieldHere's why the other options are displayed values while entering:(A) List box: When you click on a list box, a list of options appears for you to choose from. The chosen value is then displayed in the list box.(B) Text box: When you click on a text box, you can type in your own value. This value is then displayed in the text box.(C) Combo box: A combo box combines a text box and a list box. You can either type in your own value or choose a value from the list. The chosen or typed value is then displayed in the combo box.Text field is generally not a selectable element where you can input data. It’s more of a display area for already existing text dataText field

questions.text: Which of these is not a parameter in a report?
questions.subject: Computer
questions.explanation: Answer: (C) Mode of informationExplanation:Extent of information: This refers to the amount of detail included in the report.Quality of information: This refers to the accuracy, completeness, and relevance of the information in the report.Ability to acquire information: This refers to the writer's skill in gathering the necessary information for the report.Mode of information (incorrect): This is not typically considered a parameter of a report. It might be relevant to consider how the information will be presented (e.g., charts, graphs, tables), but this would be part of the overall presentation of the report, not a parameter to evaluate.

questions.text: Which is used to display yes/no, true/false or on/off options, it is used when the user can select one or multiple operations at the same time?
questions.subject: Computer
questions.explanation: Answer: C) Radio ButtonExplanation:Radio buttons are used to display a set of mutually exclusive options, meaning only one option can be selected at a time. This is ideal for yes/no, true/false, or on/off selections where only one state applies.Control objects is a general term for any element in a user interface that allows users to interact with the system. It doesn't refer to a specific button type for selecting options.Checkboxes are used to select multiple options from a set. They are not mutually exclusive, so users can select any combination of checkboxes in a group. This makes them inappropriate for scenarios where only one option should be chosen.Combo boxes can be used to display a list of options from which users can select one option. However, some combo boxes also allow users to enter their own custom value. This makes them less suitable for situations where a strict selection from a predefined set of options is required.In conclusion, radio buttons are the best choice for presenting yes/no, true/false, or on/off options because they ensure only one selection can be made at a time

questions.text: Which of the following is not a form of control objects?
questions.subject: Computer
questions.explanation: nswer: (C) FilesExplanation:Text boxes: These are used to input and edit text data.Labels: These are used to display text or instructions but do not allow user input.Combo boxes: These allow users to select from a predefined list of options or enter their own data.Files (incorrect): While files can be used to store data used by forms, they are not considered control objects themselves. Control objects are interactive elements within a form that users can interact with to provide input or view information. Files are a separate entity that may be used as a data source for forms

questions.text: Which shortcut key is used to print a report?
questions.subject: Computer
questions.explanation: Answer: (D) Ctrl+PExplanation:Ctrl+P is the universally recognized keyboard shortcut for printing a document or report across various applications. It opens the print dialog box, allowing you to specify printing options like the number of copies, printer selection, and page orientation before initiating the print process.Ctrl+N is a common shortcut used to create a new document or file in many programs. It's not related to printing.Ctrl+F is typically used for search functions within applications. It helps locate specific text or phrases within the document.Alt+P might be a custom shortcut specific to certain software. However, Ctrl+P is the more standard and widely used shortcut for printing.

questions.text: A formatted way to get output from a database is called:
questions.subject: Computer
questions.explanation: Answer: B. QueryExplanation:A query is a formatted statement used to retrieve specific data from a database. It allows you to filter, sort, and manipulate data based on your requirements. Queries are essential for extracting meaningful information from databases.Form is a user interface element that allows users to input and edit data in a database. While forms are used to interact with databases, they are not the formatted way to retrieve data itself.Table is a fundamental structure in a relational database that stores data in rows and columns. Tables hold the actual data, but queries are needed to extract and format that data for viewing or analysis.Report is a presentation layer that displays data retrieved from a database in a user-friendly format. Reports often combine data from multiple tables and queries to provide a comprehensive view of the information.

questions.text: What are MS Access forms designed to do
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: This is the correct answer. MS Access forms are primarily designed to allow users to input data into a database
While users can enter data into forms, they are not designed to manipulate data in the same way that a query would
This is a vague term that could be interpreted in different ways. While forms do accept data, their primary function is to allow users to input data

questions.explanation: MS Access forms are a user interface element that allows users to interact with a database. Their primary function is to allow users to input data into the database. While users can view and edit data through forms, they are not the most efficient way to manipulate data in a database

questions.text: A form that contains a sub form is called
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: general terms for data collection tools and don't specifically refer to forms containing sub forms
Main Form refers to the primary form that contains the sub form
general terms for data collection tools and don't specifically refer to forms containing sub forms

A subform is a form that is embedded within another form. The outer form is called the main form or parent form, and the inner form is called the subform or child form. Subforms are useful for displaying related data from multiple tables or queries in a single view. For example, a customer order form might have a subform that displays all the items for that particular order.Here are some additional points to note about subforms:● Subforms are linked to the main form using a linking field. This linking field ensures that the data displayed in the subform is relevant to the current record in the main form.● Subforms can be used to add, edit, and delete data in the child table or query.● Subforms can be helpful for improving data entry efficiency and reducing errors

questions.explanation: A subform is a form that is embedded within another form. The outer form is called the main form or parent form, and the inner form is called the subform or child form. Subforms are useful for displaying related data from multiple tables or queries in a single view. For example, a customer order form might have a subform that displays all the items for that particular order.Here are some additional points to note about subforms:● Subforms are linked to the main form using a linking field. This linking field ensures that the data displayed in the subform is relevant to the current record in the main form.● Subforms can be used to add, edit, and delete data in the child table or query.● Subforms can be helpful for improving data entry efficiency and reducing errors

options.explanation: There are more than two basic layouts
There are more than three basic layouts

There are four basic layouts of forms in MS Access:Columnar: Aligns all fields in columns.Tabular: Creates a form that looks like a table.Datasheet: Creates a form that looks like a datasheet.Justified: Creates a form where all records are justified to one side
There are only four basic layouts

questions.text: A report that provides a column for each field of the records in rows under the column header is known as
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: While similar to columnar reports, tabular reports can also have rows representing categories and columns representing specific data points. So, tabular reports offer more flexibility in data organization

A columnar report organizes data in a way that each field (or column) represents a specific category of information, and each record (or row) displays the details for that category. This format is ideal for comparing data points across different records

This term is not commonly used in database terminology. A datasheet is the primary view for tables in database management systems, where data is entered and edited directly. Reports, on the other hand, are formatted presentations of data, often drawing information from multiple tables

A justified report refers to the alignment of text within the report. Text can be left-aligned, right-aligned, or justified (aligned to both margins). Justification is a formatting option and not a specific report layout

questions.explanation: A columnar report organizes data in a way that each field (or column) represents a specific category of information, and each record (or row) displays the details for that category. This format is ideal for comparing data points across different records

questions.text: In MS-Access a report may be based on
questions.subject: Computer

options.text: A table but not a query
options.explanation: While reports can be based on tables, they can also be based on queries

options.text: A query but not a table
options.explanation: While reports can be based on queries, they can also be based on tables
Forms are primarily used for data entry and editing in MS Access. While reports can be generated from data displayed in forms, reports themselves are not based on forms

In MS Access, reports can be created using underlying data from either tables or queries.● Tables: Tables store the raw data in your database. Reports based on tables directly display all fields and records from the chosen table.● Queries: Queries are like filtered views of your tables. You can use queries to select specific fields, filter data based on criteria, and perform calculations before feeding the results into a report. This allows for more customized report generation.

questions.explanation: In MS Access, reports can be created using underlying data from either tables or queries.● Tables: Tables store the raw data in your database. Reports based on tables directly display all fields and records from the chosen table.● Queries: Queries are like filtered views of your tables. You can use queries to select specific fields, filter data based on criteria, and perform calculations before feeding the results into a report. This allows for more customized report generation.

Reports are used to summarize and present data from a database in a user-friendly format. They can be printed, saved as a file, or viewed on screen

questions.text: A single report can display data from
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: ● While reports can be generated from a single table, relational databases are designed to store data in multiple tables with relationships between them. Reports that combine data from multiple tables are often more informative and useful.● Option (a) is incorrect because reports can be more powerful when they leverage data from multiple tables.● Option (b) is not entirely wrong, but it's not the most precise answer. Reports can be generated from one table, but using multiple tables is more common.● Option (d) is incorrect because reports are designed to display data from a database, and relational databases typically consist of multiple tables.In conclusion, reports can be generated from one table, but reports that display data from at least two tables are more common and powerful in relational databases

options.explanation: Tabular reports also display data in rows and columns, but they can group or categorize data and offer more flexibility in layout compared to datasheet reports
Summary reports focus on presenting aggregated data, such as counts, averages, or sums, rather than listing every single field value. They are used for gaining insights and trends from the data

A datasheet report displays data in a similar format to a datasheet view in MS Access. Each record occupies a row, and each field is displayed in a separate column. This format is useful for quickly browsing through large datasets without additional formatting or grouping

Columnar reports organize data with each category (field) having its own dedicated column, while each record details are spread across rows. This is different from a datasheet report where all fields are listed in a single column for each record

questions.text: If you want to track the progress of the stock market on a daily basis, which type of chart should you use
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: A line chart is the best way to visualize the daily ups and downs of the stock market. It uses a line to connect data points, allowing you to easily see how the price of a stock has changed over time. The X-axis typically represents time (days, weeks, months, etc.), and the Y-axis represents the stock price. This makes it easy to identify trends and patterns in the stock's performance.Here are some additional benefits of using line charts for tracking the stock market:● They are simple and easy to read.● They can show a lot of data in a small space.You can easily compare multiple stocks on the same chart.
useful for comparing different categories at a single point in time, but they are not ideal for tracking trends over time.
useful for comparing different categories at a single point in time, but they are not ideal for tracking trends over time.
Pie chart is typically used to represent proportions of a whole at a single point in time. It is not well-suited for showing trends over time

questions.explanation: A line chart is the best way to visualize the daily ups and downs of the stock market. It uses a line to connect data points, allowing you to easily see how the price of a stock has changed over time. The X-axis typically represents time (days, weeks, months, etc.), and the Y-axis represents the stock price. This makes it easy to identify trends and patterns in the stock's performance.Here are some additional benefits of using line charts for tracking the stock market:● They are simple and easy to read.● They can show a lot of data in a small space.You can easily compare multiple stocks on the same chart.

In MS Access, you can create subforms using several methods:Form Wizard: The form wizard provides a step-by-step interface to guide you through creating a subform with predefined layouts and functionalities.Subform Wizard (not shown in all MS Access versions): Some versions of MS Access offer a dedicated subform wizard specifically designed to simplify the process of creating subforms within a main form.Drag and Drop Method: You can drag and drop fields from the main form or the data source directly onto the main form to create a subform. questions.text: What is the function of a form in MS Access
questions.subject: Computer

options.text: View one record at a time
options.explanation: View one record at a time is a common function of forms, but forms can also be designed to display multiple records
﻿Change existing data allows users to modify existing information in the database
Add new data enables users to enter new records into the database

Forms are a vital user interface element in Microsoft Access. They provide a way to interact with data in your database, including:● Viewing data: Forms can be designed to display data from a single record or multiple records at a time. You can customize the layout of the form to make the data easier to read and understand.● Adding new data: Forms provide a user-friendly interface for entering new data into your database. You can add controls such as text boxes, combo boxes, and date pickers to make data entry efficient and accurate.● Changing existing data: You can use forms to modify existing data in your database. This is helpful for updating records or correcting errors.In conclusion, forms are versatile tools that can be used for various tasks in Microsoft Access, making them essential for effective database interaction

questions.text: Which view can be used to enter or modify data in the underlying table
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: Form view is specifically designed for entering and modifying data in a user-friendly way. It provides an interface with labeled fields and data entry controls, making it easier to add, edit, or delete records compared to the datasheet view

This view allows you to customize the layout and appearance of a form, but you cannot directly enter or modify data in this view

questions.text: When displaying a sum from within a main form, which form is used to display primary values
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: The sum needs to be displayed on one of the forms

The main form is typically used to display primary values, while a subform can be used to display related data from another table or query. The sum function would be used within the main form to calculate the total based on the values displayed in the main form
While a subform can display data, it's not typically used to display the primary values used for calculating the sum
A separate form is not necessary in this scenario. The main form can handle both displaying the primary values and calculating the sum
The main form is the most likely candidate to display the primary values, while subforms and separate forms have other purposes

questions.explanation: The main form is typically used to display primary values, while a subform can be used to display related data from another table or query. The sum function would be used within the main form to calculate the total based on the values displayed in the main form

questions.text: Which of the following options is used for editing forms in MS Access
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: can be used to help you align and position objects on a form in Layout View or Design View. They are not essential for editing forms but can be helpful for creating a well-organized and visually appealing form

can be used to help you align and position objects on a form in Layout View or Design View. They are not essential for editing forms but can be helpful for creating a well-organized and visually appealing form
Resizing objects is a fundamental aspect of editing forms. You can resize controls such as text boxes, labels, and buttons to fit your needs and create the desired layout for your form

Microsoft Access offers several tools and techniques for editing forms in Layout View and Design View. Here's a breakdown of the options mentioned:● Grid lines and Snap to Grid: These are visual aids that can assist you in precisely positioning controls on your form. Grid lines appear as dotted lines on the form's background, and Snap to Grid will automatically nudge objects to align with the grid or other controls when you move them. While not strictly necessary for editing forms, they can make the process more efficient and ensure a professional look for your form.● Resizing objects: This is a core function of editing forms in Access. You can directly resize controls by dragging their edges or using the size properties in the property sheet. Resizing allows you to adjust the size of text boxes, labels, buttons, and other elements to fit the content you want to display and optimize the form's usability.In conclusion, all of the above options - Grid lines, Snap to Grid, and Resizing objects - play a role in editing forms in MS Access. They can help you achieve the desired layout, improve the accuracy of your form design, and enhance the overall user experience

questions.text: Which of the following is used to display a list of items in forms
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: A list box displays a list of options and users can select one or multiple items (depending on how it's set up)
A combo box also displays a list, but it often combines a list box with a text box where users can enter a value not on the list

● List box and Combo box are both input controls used in forms to allow users to select items from a list.o A list box displays a list of options and users can select one or multiple items (depending on how it's set up).o A combo box also displays a list, but it often combines a list box with a text box where users can enter a value not on the list.

Radio buttons are used to select only one option from a set of mutually exclusive choices. They are typically circular and clicking one button deselects any other selected radio button in the same group. So, they are not designed to display a general list of items

questions.text: All of the following are forms layouts EXCEPT
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: Tabular layout organizes data in rows and columns, similar to a spreadsheet. This is a common way to view and enter multiple records at once
Columnar layout arranges data in vertical columns, which can be useful for specific situations where you want to prioritize reading information down a page rather than across rows
Justified aligns text within a control to the left and right margins, creating a flush look at the edges. It can be used to format text fields within a form

Relationship layout is not a standard form layout option in Microsoft Access. Relationships are created between tables in the database schema to define how data is linked between them. They are not a way to format how data is displayed in a form. In conclusion, Tabular, Columnar, and Justified are all valid form layout options used to control how data is displayed in a form, while Relationship refers to the connections between tables in the database schema and not how data is formatted within a form

questions.explanation: Relationship layout is not a standard form layout option in Microsoft Access. Relationships are created between tables in the database schema to define how data is linked between them. They are not a way to format how data is displayed in a form. In conclusion, Tabular, Columnar, and Justified are all valid form layout options used to control how data is displayed in a form, while Relationship refers to the connections between tables in the database schema and not how data is formatted within a form

questions.text: Which of the following is used to display values?
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: A checkbox is typically used to indicate a yes/no or on/off option. It can be selected or deselected to represent a binary state

Option buttons are used to select one option from a set of mutually exclusive choices. They are typically circular and clicking one button deselects any other selected radio button in the same group. While they can display text labels for the options, the option button itself doesn't inherently display a value

Text box is a fundamental input control in MS Access forms. It allows users to enter and display alphanumeric data (letters, numbers, and symbols) in a single line. Text boxes are used for various purposes in forms, such as entering names, addresses, descriptions, or any other data point that can be represented as text

questions.text: You can drag which of the following bars to move the property sheet window around on your screen
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: This is the bar at the top of the property sheet window. You can click and drag the title bar to move the window anywhere on your screen

This is the bar at the bottom of the property sheet window. While it typically doesn't allow dragging to move the window in Access, it might offer additional information or context depending on the application

If the property sheet content extends beyond the visible area of the window, a scroll bar will appear on the right side. You can click and drag the scroll bar thumb or use the scroll arrows to move up and down within the property sheet, revealing more properties if needed

In Microsoft Access, the property sheet is a window that displays the properties of an object or control in a form or report. You can use the properties to customize the appearance and behavior of the object. The property sheet has different sections to organize the properties, and you might need to use the scroll bar to see all the properties

questions.text: Which box values are all displayed during data entering
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: Radio buttons are used to select only one option from a group of mutually exclusive choices. During data entry, only the selected radio button value is displayed
This term is not commonly used in user interface design. It might be referring to radio buttons, which as mentioned earlier, only display the selected value during data entry.

questions.text: The printed or screen display of data or information in the database is called
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: Entity refers to a fundamental unit of data in a relational database model, representing a real-world object, concept, or event. It doesn't represent the output display of data

In Microsoft Access, a report is a formatted presentation of data retrieved from your database. Reports are designed to be printed or viewed electronically and are often used for summarizing, analyzing, or sharing information. They allow you to organize and present data in a user-friendly way, including:● Grouping and sorting data● Applying formatting and layout elements like headers, footers, and titles● Including calculations and summariesReports are a valuable tool for transforming raw data from your database into clear and informative output that can be easily understood by users.

Query is a temporary result set created from database tables based on specific criteria. While queries are used to retrieve and manipulate data, they are not the final output format for presenting information

Screen is a general term for the display surface and doesn't specifically refer to a database output format. Reports can be printed or viewed on a screen, but "report" is the more precise term in this context

questions.explanation: In Microsoft Access, a report is a formatted presentation of data retrieved from your database. questions.text: Which of the following provides a different way of viewing table data?
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: Forms are user interfaces that allow users to interact with data in a database. They typically use input controls like text boxes, combo boxes, and radio buttons to facilitate data entry, editing, and deletio
Queries are used to retrieve and filter data based on specific criteria. They don't directly display data themselves, but their output can be used to populate forms and reports

Tables are the foundational structure for storing data in a relational database. They organize data into rows and columns, with each column representing a specific field and each row representing a record

Reports present data from a database in a formatted and organized way, often for printing or sharing. They can include elements like titles, headers, footers, data fields, and formatting to make the information visually appealing and easy to understand. In this sense, reports offer a different way of viewing data compared to tables and forms, which are more focused on data entry and manipulation

options.explanation: Buttons are used to initiate actions within a form, such as saving data, navigating to different records, or opening other forms. They don't directly display data for entry, but they play a crucial role in the data entry process
Check boxes allow users to select one or more options from a set of choices. They are useful for capturing boolean data (yes/no) or multiple selections for a particular field
While not as common for data entry, graphs can be used to display data visually within a form. They can be helpful for users to see trends or patterns in the data they are entering
Control objects are fundamental elements used to design and interact with forms in MS Access. They provide a way to display information, accept user input, and trigger actions within the form

questions.text: this is not one of the sections of the form design window
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: the form footer often contains concluding information such as page numbers or copyright notices

The form design window in Microsoft Access is the environment where you create and modify the layout and functionality of your forms. It consists of several distinct sections that allow you to organize the elements of your form.● Form Header: This section appears at the top of the form and is often used to display a title, logo, or other introductory information.● Form Body / Form Content: This is the main area of the form where you place controls like text boxes, labels, buttons, and other elements used for interacting with data. The question seems to be referring to this section with the term "Form Content".● Form Footer: This section appears at the bottom of the form and is commonly used for page numbers, copyright information, or other concluding remarks.By effectively utilizing these sections, you can design forms in Access that are not only functional but also visually appealing and user-friendly
The form header typically displays introductory information or a title for the form
The form detail section is where you place the main body controls for data entry, display, and manipulation

questions.text: What does the show check box in the query design window indicate
questions.subject: Computer

options.text: It indicates whether the field is used in the query or not
options.explanation: While checking the box ensures the field is used in the query, it doesn't necessarily mean it's used in calculations or joins. It could simply be displayed in the results

options.text: It indicates whether the field is displayed in the query result or not
options.explanation: The check box next to a field in the query design window controls whether that field is included in the final output of the query. When the box is checked, the field is included in the query result. When it's unchecked, the field is not displayed in the result, even though it might still be used in calculations or filtering within the query itself

options.text: It indicates whether the field name is displayed in the query result or not
options.explanation: The check box controls the field itself, not the field name. You can choose to display the field without its name in the results
None of the above is not correct. The check box does have a specific purpose related to how the field is used in the query

questions.explanation: The check box next to a field in the query design window controls whether that field is included in the final output of the query. When the box is checked, the field is included in the query result. When it's unchecked, the field is not displayed in the result, even though it might still be used in calculations or filtering within the query itself

questions.text: Which box values are not displayed until the arrow button is clicked open
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: List boxes display all available options at all times. Users can scroll through the list to see all choices and select the desired one
(Also commonly called radio buttons): Option boxes display all available choices at all times. However, only one option can be selected at a time

In contrast, combo boxes typically show a limited preview of the available options in a text box. Clicking the arrow button expands the list, revealing all choices for selection. So, the full list of values in a combo box is not displayed until the user interacts with it

questions.text: What is a form that we can create in MS Access
questions.subject: Computer

options.text: It is used to print out of a form from Access Tables
options.explanation: This is incorrect. While reports are designed for printing and formatted output, forms are primarily for data entry and interaction

options.text: It is an input screen for viewing and entering data
options.explanation: This is the correct answer. Forms are a crucial component of MS Access for interacting with data in a user-friendly way. They provide a graphical interface with controls like text boxes, combo boxes, and buttons that allow users to:o View existing data records from tables or querieso Add new data recordso Edit existing data recordso Delete data records

options.text: It is used for storing special data other than text (e.g photo)
options.explanation: This is incorrect. While attachments or OLE objects can be incorporated into forms, it's not their primary function. Storing special data is typically handled by specific data types in tables or through attachments linked to records

options.text: It is optional. We can use Report instead of Form
options.explanation: This is incorrect. Reports and forms serve different purposes. Reports are designed for presenting data in a formatted way for printing or sharing, while forms are for data entry and manipulation. You can use both reports and forms in an Access database depending on your needs

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a parameter in a report
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: Extent of information refers to the comprehensiveness of the data included in the report.
Quality of information refers to the accuracy, relevance, and reliability of the data presented in the report

While there can be many factors to consider when creating a report, the age of the writer is not typically a direct parameter that influences the report itself. The focus is on the content and its quality, not the characteristics of the person who wrote it.Here are some additional parameters you might consider when designing a report:● Clarity and conciseness: The report should be easy to understand and avoid unnecessary redundancy.● Target audience: The report should be tailored to the needs and interests of the intended readers.● Formatting and presentation: The report should be visually appealing and well-organized to enhance readability.By carefully considering these parameters, you can create effective reports that communicate information clearly and achieve their desired outcome
Ability to acquire information considers the ease with which the report author can gather the necessary data to create the report﻿

questions.text: Which of the following can compare, summarize, and substitute large sets of data
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: Table is a fundamental data structure in a relational database that stores data in rows and columns. While tables can hold large amounts of data, they are not specifically designed for comparing, summarizing, or substituting information

Form is a user interface element in Access used for data entry, editing, and display. Forms can present data from a table, but they are not ideal for complex comparisons, summaries, or substitutions of large datasets

Query is a temporary result set created from database tables based on specific criteria. Queries can be used to retrieve, filter, and manipulate data, but they don't provide the level of summarization and presentation typically required for reports

In Microsoft Access, a report is a formatted presentation of data retrieved from your database. Reports are designed to analyze, summarize, and present large sets of data in a user-friendly way. They offer several functionalities that make them suitable for working with extensive data:Grouping and sorting: Reports can group and sort data according to various criteria, allowing you to identify patterns, trends, and relationships within the information.Calculations and summaries: Reports can perform calculations and display summaries of your data, such as averages, counts, and totals. This helps you condense large datasets into meaningful insights.Formatting and presentation: Reports allow for formatting and layout customization, including headers, footers, titles, and visual elements. This improves readability and helps users understand the presented information more easily
